message:
feat(security): add ZoneBound<T> type-level zone binding invariant [MOR/C3.7]

ZoneBound<T> wraps a value with an immutable ZoneId binding. Access
requires zone match via with_zone_check(), preventing accidental
cross-zone operations at the API level. Includes Serialize/Deserialize,
PartialEq/Eq, mutable access, and consuming unwrap. 8 acceptance tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Run the shared connector compliance runner and capture an evidence bundle.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-4zr0","title":"[IDEA-21] Zone Policy Diff + Rollback Tooling","description":"## Background
Zone policies change over time. Operators need a safe way to review diffs and roll back with clear evidence and approvals.

## Goals
- Compute diffs between ZonePolicy/ZoneDefinition objects.
- Generate rollback plans and highlight risky changes.
- Expose CLI output for audits and CI.

## Non-goals
- Automatic policy edits without approval.

## Approach
- Implement a structured diff engine for zone policy objects.
- Add CLI `fcp policy diff` and `fcp policy rollback --plan`.
- Require ApprovalToken for rollbacks in high-trust zones.

## Risks
- Diff noise from ordering changes; enforce canonical ordering before diff.

## Success Criteria
- Operators can see precise changes to capabilities, transport policy, and ceilings.
- Rollback outputs are deterministic and auditable.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Diff flags risk‑increasing changes (capabilities, transport policy).
- Rollback plan is deterministic and auditable.
","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add spectral routing score (effective resistance / Laplacian) as optional metric and log chosen path.
- Set gossip TTL from measured mixing-time; add convergence test that asserts bound.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Use SmallVec for usually-small object/symbol lists to reduce allocations.
- Batch symbol requests and use Bytes for zero-copy in hot paths.
[AlienArtifactReview] Updated 2026-02-02T23:58:59.240755+00:00","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:44:10.503006401Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T05:56:33.169068635Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T05:56:33.168990219Z","close_reason":"All child tasks complete: flywheel_connectors-23d7 (CLI diff/rollback), flywheel_connectors-3dl4 (unit tests), flywheel_connectors-69vu (E2E rollback).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-503","title":"fcp.terraform: Drift detection between state and actual resources","description":"# fcp.terraform: Drift detection between state and actual resources (FCP2)

## Goal
Detect drift safely by comparing:
- recorded Terraform state
- actual provider-reported infrastructure

This may require provider API egress and must respect NetworkConstraints.

## Scope
- Run a refresh-only plan (no apply) and extract drift summary.
- Return a canonical `DriftReport`:
  - resources changed
  - attributes changed (hashed/summary)
  - severity estimation

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `terraform.drift.read` (sensitive)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints default deny unless backend/providers are explicitly allowed.
- No mutations; drift detection must not apply changes.
- Audit state/provider access.

## Deliverables
- Operation schema:
  - `terraform.drift.detect`
- Canonical output type:
  - `DriftReport`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Unit tests with a terraform shim for:
  - refresh-only invocation
  - parsing drift summaries
  - error mapping

## Acceptance criteria
- Drift reports are deterministic and safe-by-default.

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-50gzx","title":"[MOR/C3.2] Implement proof-carrying taint reduction via phantom types","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:34:55.543501Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-10T23:04:18.659742Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["provenance","security","type-safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-50gzx","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-8whb3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:37:05.276925Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2641,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-50gzx","author":"jemanuel","text":"WHAT: Replace TaintFlags::remove() (which has a comment-only proof requirement) with a proof-carrying taint reduction system using phantom types. Taint flags can only be reduced by constructing a TaintReduction struct that references a SanitizerReceipt (a first-class mesh object proving the sanitization happened). WHY: A7 Type-Theoretic found that taint reduction currently has no enforcement — TaintFlags::remove() requires a SanitizerReceipt by COMMENT but not by API. A programmer could clear PUBLIC_INPUT taint without any proof and the compiler would allow it. This is a provenance violation: if tainted data appears clean without proof, the entire provenance lattice is untrustworthy. IMPLEMENTATION SKETCH: ```rust pub struct TaintReduction { pub sanitizer_receipt_id: ObjectId, pub cleared_flags: Vec<TaintFlag>, pub sanitizer_capability: CapabilityId, } impl TaintFlags { // Remove the bare remove() method entirely // Replace with: pub fn reduce_with_proof(mut self, reduction: &TaintReduction) -> Result<Self, TaintError> { // Verify reduction references valid flags for flag in &reduction.cleared_flags { if !self.0.contains(flag) { return Err(TaintError::FlagNotPresent(*flag)); } self.0.remove(flag); } // Record the reduction in provenance Ok(self) } // Keep add() — adding taint never needs proof pub fn add(&mut self, flag: TaintFlag) { self.0.insert(flag); } } ``` WHERE: crates/fcp-core/src/provenance.rs — TaintFlags, TaintFlag, TaintReduction. The SanitizerReceipt type may already exist or may need creation in crates/fcp-core/src/revocation.rs or a new file. ACCEPTANCE: (1) TaintFlags::remove() is deleted. (2) reduce_with_proof() requires TaintReduction with receipt reference. (3) TaintReduction is serializable and auditable. (4) Provenance carries taint_reductions: Vec<TaintReduction> (may already exist). (5) At least 5 tests: reduce with valid proof succeeds, reduce without proof compile error (no remove method), reduce non-present flag fails, reduction recorded in provenance, serialization round-trip. ESTIMATED EFFORT: 2-3 days.","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:36:48Z"}]}

Taint flags can only be reduced by constructing a TaintReduction struct that references a SanitizerReceipt (a first-class mesh object proving the sanitization happened). WHY: A7 Type-Theoretic found that taint reduction currently has no enforcement — TaintFlags::remove() requires a SanitizerReceipt by COMMENT but not by API. A programmer could clear PUBLIC_INPUT taint without any proof and the compiler would allow it. This is a provenance violation: if tainted data appears clean without proof, the entire provenance lattice is untrustworthy. IMPLEMENTATION SKETCH: ```rust pub struct TaintReduction { pub sanitizer_receipt_id: ObjectId, pub cleared_flags: Vec<TaintFlag>, pub sanitizer_capability: CapabilityId, } impl TaintFlags { // Remove the bare remove() method entirely // Replace with: pub fn reduce_with_proof(mut self, reduction: &TaintReduction) -> Result<Self, TaintError> { // Verify reduction references valid flags for flag in &reduction.cleared_flags { if !self.0.contains(flag) { return Err(TaintError::FlagNotPresent(*flag)); } self.0.remove(flag); } // Record the reduction in provenance Ok(self) } // Keep add() — adding taint never needs proof pub fn add(&mut self, flag: TaintFlag) { self.0.insert(flag); } } ``` WHERE: crates/fcp-core/src/provenance.rs — TaintFlags, TaintFlag, TaintReduction. The SanitizerReceipt type may already exist or may need creation in crates/fcp-core/src/revocation.rs or a new file. ACCEPTANCE: (1) TaintFlags::remove() is deleted. (2) reduce_with_proof() requires TaintReduction with receipt reference. (3) TaintReduction is serializable and auditable. (4) Provenance carries taint_reductions: Vec<TaintReduction> (may already exist). (5) At least 5 tests: reduce with valid proof succeeds, reduce without proof compile error (no remove method), reduce non-present flag fails, reduction recorded in provenance, serialization round-trip. ESTIMATED EFFORT: 2-3 days.","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:36:48Z"},{"id":2659,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-50gzx","author":"jemanuel","text":"C3.2 complete: (1) TaintFlags::remove() deleted, (2) reduce_with_proof() takes &TaintReduction with sanitizer_receipt_id, (3) TaintReduction already Serialize+Deserialize, (4) ProvenanceRecord.taint_reductions: Vec<TaintReduction> already exists, (5) 8 new tests (6 unit + 2 integration). Commit 049ce865.","created_at":"2026-04-10T23:13:21Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-50oeb","title":"Plaid: remove sandbox.plaid.com default, require explicit environment selection","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BronzeStork","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:23.486164Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T04:03:53.136715Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:46:47.573271Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","critical","hardening"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-50oeb","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qgu6t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:35.635636Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1940,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-50oeb","author":"jemanuel","text":"File: connectors/plaid/src/client.rs:21
Current: const DEFAULT_BASE_URL: &str = "https://sandbox.plaid.com";

Also: connectors/plaid/src/connector.rs:18 defines SANDBOX_BASE_URL separately and connector.rs:65 has a PlaidEnvironment enum already partially implemented with Sandbox/Development/Production variants.

Steps:
1. Remove DEFAULT_BASE_URL from client.rs (line 21)
2. Complete the PlaidEnvironment enum already in connector.rs — make it the canonical way to select environment
3. Map environments: Sandbox -> sandbox.plaid.com, Development -> development.plaid.com, Production -> production.plaid.com
4. Update PlaidClient::new() to require environment as a non-optional parameter
5. Update configure() to parse environment from config, reject if missing
6. Add doctor() check that reports active environment and warns if sandbox
7. Update self_check() to verify environment URL is reachable
8. Update tests (including client.rs:806 which explicitly asserts sandbox default)
9. Verify: cargo test -p fcp-plaid passes

Note: Plaid already has partial environment support in connector.rs — this task completes it and removes the unsafe default.","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:35Z"},{"id":1967,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-50oeb","author":"BronzeStork","text":"Claiming this slice via fresh-eyes audit. I reserved connectors/plaid/src/client.rs and connectors/plaid/src/connector.rs to eliminate the remaining sandbox default at the client boundary and align the constructor/configuration/tests with explicit environment selection.","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:45:18Z"},{"id":1969,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-50oeb","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose completed: Made environment required in PlaidConfig::from_params() (no longer defaults to sandbox), renamed DEFAULT_BASE_URL to SANDBOX_BASE_URL in client.rs, updated all tests (224 lib + integration) to explicitly provide environment. 18 pre-existing integration test failures (OperationNotGranted from capability verification) are unrelated to this change.","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:46:47Z"},{"id":1971,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-50oeb","author":"BronzeStork","text":"Completed the Plaid hardening slice. Removed the remaining silent sandbox default from PlaidClient::new(), require and validate an explicit base_url at the client boundary, updated connector configuration to pass the already-normalized URL directly, and migrated the Plaid unit/integration tests off the with_base_url footgun. Verification: rch exec -- cargo fmt --check -p fcp-plaid passed; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-plaid-bronzestork cargo test -p fcp-plaid --no-run passed remotely in 3m48s. A smaller runtime smoke test was attempted through rch as well, but worker churn/local fail-open issues made it inconclusive after the build gate was already green.","created_at":"2026-03-25T04:03:53Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-51o","title":"fcp.airtable: Linked record resolution and management","description":"# fcp.airtable: Linked Record Resolution and Management

## Goal
Provide first-class support for Airtable relational features:
- linked record fields
- lookup/rollup-style derived values

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Scope
- Read/write “link to another record” fields (arrays of record ids).
- Resolve linked record chains for agent-friendly outputs (bounded joins).
- Support computed field read semantics (lookup/rollup) without pretending they are writable.

## Key requirements
- Capability gated (reads vs writes).
- Bounded traversal:
  - prevent unbounded graph walks / cycles
  - explicit depth/record count limits
- Deterministic output:
  - stable ordering when expanding linked records
  - explicit “partial” indicators when traversal is truncated by bounds

## Tests
- Linked graph traversal (including cycles).
- Bounded expansion behavior.
- Error mapping for missing/deleted linked records.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Linked record expansion is safe and predictable.
- Computed fields are exposed read-only with clear typing.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:14:36.301383823Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T01:47:36.938011Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T01:47:36.937990Z","close_reason":"Implemented bounded linked-record expansion, field metadata, manifest updates, and coverage; verified with rch-backed fmt/check/test/clippy for fcp-airtable","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2","tier2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-51o","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-soft","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-51o","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-soft.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-556f","title":"TEST: RaptorQ Unit Tests (Encode/Decode, Chunking, DoS Bounds)","description":"# TEST: RaptorQ Unit Tests


## Goal
Prove RaptorQ encode/decode correctness and enforce decode DoS bounds (size/time) with reproducible test vectors.

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for RaptorQ symbol encoding/decoding and chunking.

## Test Categories

### Basic Encoding/Decoding
- [ ] Encode small object (< symbol size) → decode from K symbols
- [ ] Encode medium object (multiple symbols) → decode from K symbols
- [ ] Encode large object → ChunkedObjectManifest path
- [ ] Decode with exactly K source symbols
- [ ] Decode with K + repair symbols (erasure recovery)
- [ ] Decode with subset of repair symbols only

### Symbol Parameters
- [ ] Default symbol size (1024 bytes) produces expected symbol count
- [ ] Custom symbol sizes work correctly
- [ ] K (source symbols) calculation is correct for object sizes
- [ ] Repair ratio produces expected number of repair symbols

### ChunkedObjectManifest
- [ ] Objects > threshold produce manifest + raw chunks
- [ ] Manifest references correct chunk ObjectIds
- [ ] Chunks reconstruct to original object
- [ ] Chunk ordering is deterministic

### Decode DoS Mitigation
- [ ] Reject decode attempts exceeding CPU budget
- [ ] Reject decode attempts exceeding memory budget
- [ ] Timeout on pathological decode patterns
- [ ] Reject impossibly large symbol counts
- [ ] Reject impossibly large object sizes in manifest

### Symbol Identity
- [ ] Same object always produces same symbols (deterministic)
- [ ] ESI (encoding symbol identifier) is unique per symbol
- [ ] Symbols are self-describing (include ESI, K)

### Partial Availability
- [ ] DecodeStatus correctly reports received vs needed
- [ ] SymbolAck correctly acknowledges specific ESIs
- [ ] Missing symbol requests are targeted (not full retransmit)

## Golden Vectors
- 1KB object → expected symbols (with specific bytes)
- 100KB object → expected manifest structure
- Known erasure pattern → successful decode

## Fuzz Targets
- Malformed symbol streams
- Invalid ESI values
- Oversized object claims
- Corrupt manifest structures

## Performance Benchmarks
- Encode 1MB object: target < 10ms
- Decode 1MB from K symbols: target < 20ms
- Decode 1MB with 10% erasure: target < 50ms

## Logging Requirements
All tests emit:
```json
{
  "test_name": "...",
  "object_size": 12345,
  "symbol_size": 1024,
  "k": 13,
  "repair_symbols": 3,
  "decode_symbols_used": 13,
  "result": "pass|fail",
  "duration_us": 1234
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All encoding/decoding paths tested
- DoS mitigations verified
- Performance benchmarks pass
- Golden vectors match

","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T07:54:19.409701172Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T21:13:34.218371332Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T21:13:34.218371332Z","close_reason":"Implemented comprehensive RaptorQ unit tests with 79 passing tests covering:
- Golden vector tests for 1KB, 10KB, 100KB, and 300KB objects
- Symbol count verification (K source + repair ratio)
- Encode/decode roundtrip for all sizes
- Erasure recovery tests (10% loss, repair-only decode)
- Symbol determinism tests (same payload = same symbols)
- ESI uniqueness verification
- DoS mitigation tests (oversized payloads, concurrent limits, timeouts, memory/symbol buffer limits)
- Decode status tracking tests
- Chunked manifest tests (ID determinism, ordering, hash verification)
All acceptance criteria met.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp2","mvp","raptorq","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-556f","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-556f","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
